data.title: Becton Dickinson raises EPS guidance amid strategic separation and innovation launches
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Becton, Dickinson and Company (BDX) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Thomas E. Polen reported sequential improvement in growth, stating "We accelerated commercial initiatives and increased our organic growth trajectory through market headwinds. We continued our strong track record of executing BD Excellence to drive gross margin upside." He highlighted the announcement of a definitive agreement to separate the Biosciences and Diagnostics business through a transaction with Waters, expected to unlock value for shareholders. Polen noted, "Revenue grew 8.5% to $5.5 billion or 3% organic. New BD organic growth was 4%." He further added, "Q3 adjusted gross margin of 54.8%, up 50 basis points year-over-year. Adjusted operating margin of 25.8%, up 60 basis points year-over-year and supported delivering $3.68 in adjusted diluted EPS, each exceeded our expectations."
  • Polen emphasized strategic initiatives, mentioning, "We announced that we entered into an agreement to combine our Biosciences and Diagnostic Solutions business with Waters through a tax-efficient Reverse Morris Trust." He also introduced Brooke Story as head of integration and separation management, citing her experience as President of the Diagnostics business.
  • CFO Christopher J. DelOrefice stated, "Revenues of $5.5 billion grew 8.5% or 3% organic. New BD organic growth was 4%. Regionally, total company organic growth was led by performance in the U.S. and Greater Asia outside of China, partially offset by China." He added, "We delivered strong adjusted gross margin of 54.8% and adjusted operating margin of 25.8%, which increased by 50 and 60 basis points year-over-year, respectively. Gross margin expansion continued to be primarily fueled by momentum in BD Excellence, driven by manufacturing productivity, waste improvement and network optimization."

Outlook

  • The company reaffirmed its organic revenue guidance range for the year and raised its earnings guidance by $0.18 at the midpoint. Polen said, "We reaffirmed our organic revenue guide range for the year and raised our earnings guidance $0.18 at the midpoint."
  • DelOrefice stated, "We raised our adjusted EPS guidance by $0.18 at the midpoint to a range of $14.30 to $14.45. This reflects growth of about 9.4% at the midpoint, which is an increase of approximately 1.4% compared to our prior guidance." He added, "We remain on track to deliver our goal of 25% operating margin by 2025."
  • The company expects a full year 2026 tariff impact of around $275 million, an improvement compared to initial expectations.

Financial Results

  • Revenue for Q3 was $5.5 billion, with organic growth at 3% and new BD organic growth at 4%.
  • Adjusted gross margin was 54.8%, and adjusted operating margin was 25.8%.
  • Adjusted diluted EPS was $3.68, growing 5.1% year-over-year.
  • Year-to-date free cash flows were approximately $1.7 billion, increasing sequentially by $1 billion.
  • Net leverage ended the quarter at 2.8x, with progress towards a 2.5x long-term target.
  • The company expects to complete its $1 billion share buyback by the end of September.

Q&A

  • Patrick Wood, Morgan Stanley, asked about RemainCo's growth framework and mid-single-digit plus growth potential. Polen responded, "There's nothing different that we would expect fundamentally from those. I think as we -- as you also mentioned, we saw very strong growth in APM ahead of our deal model, 13% growth for the quarter."
  • Frederick Allen Wise, Stifel, questioned Q4 margin dynamics and headwinds. DelOrefice replied, "There'll be a slight sequential step down quarter-to-quarter, and it's largely driven by just the timing of our investments... we're actually fueling the business with more investment to continue to fuel those areas of momentum."
  • Lawrence H. Biegelsen, Wells Fargo, inquired about margin outlook post-separation and fiscal 2026 impacts from tariffs. DelOrefice stated, "There will actually be very difference in terms of operating margin pre-separation and post separation. So that's within a reasonable range, plus or minus. So you should see a healthy margin come out, plus you're going to continue to get the benefit of BD Excellence carrying through."
  • Travis Lee Steed, Bank of America, asked about growth outlook and capital deployment post-separation. Polen explained, "We're obviously very focused on executing in '25 and chopping wood through the balance of the year, but I could give a little bit of -- and it's too early to talk details about '26, but maybe just give a little bit of thoughts to share related to that."
  • Robert Justin Marcus, JPMorgan, asked about urology growth and SG&A investments. Polen confirmed, "Now urology is continuing. There's nothing onetime there at all. It's a continuation of the trends...really driven by innovation on the UCC side." DelOrefice added, "We want to keep investing in both innovation and commercial execution."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' tone was inquisitive and focused on clarification around margin impacts, future growth, and separation details, with a neutral to slightly positive sentiment as they recognized strong execution but pressed for clarity on future risks.
  • Management maintained a confident and constructive tone in prepared remarks, using phrases such as "We are pleased with the performance" and "We feel really good about that," but showed some caution and prudence when discussing potential macro headwinds and tariff impacts.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst skepticism about macro challenges and mitigation strategies was less pronounced, and management's confidence appeared strengthened by improved sequential performance and raised guidance.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted from a defensive stance in Q2 (addressing missed topline expectations and macro challenges) to an assertive tone in Q3, with raised EPS guidance and reaffirmed revenue targets.
  • Strategic focus advanced from mitigation and stabilization (tariffs, supply chain, research funding) to active separation of the Biosciences and Diagnostics business and new product launches.
  • Analysts in Q3 shifted from probing missed targets and mitigation to exploring growth sustainability and the impact of the separation.
  • Key metric changes included improved revenue growth, margin expansion, and a higher EPS outlook. Management expressed increased confidence in operational execution and innovation-driven growth.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted persistent market headwinds in China, volatility in subsegments such as vaccines, and ongoing tariff impacts as ongoing risks.
  • The company continues to mitigate tariffs through supply chain optimization and sourcing changes, with teams deployed to "keep whittling that down."
  • Analysts raised concerns about the sustainability of margin expansion, the permanence of new investments in SG&A, and the impact of tariffs on future financials.

Final Takeaway

Becton Dickinson delivered sequential improvements in growth, expanded margins, and exceeded earnings expectations in the third quarter, driven by successful commercial initiatives and strategic investments. The company reaffirmed its revenue guidance and raised its EPS outlook, underscoring confidence in operational momentum and innovation launches. The pending separation of the Biosciences and Diagnostics business and continued focus on margin expansion through BD Excellence set the stage for BD’s next phase as a pure-play medtech leader, while proactive tariff mitigation and targeted investments support a robust foundation for long-term value creation.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Golar LNG firms up 20-year FLNG charter in Argentina
data.description:
Golar LNG (NASDAQ:GLNG) +1.4% in early trading Thursday after saying Southern Energy S.A. reached a final investment decision for the charter of Golar's 3.5M tons/year MK II floating liquefied natural gas vessel, firming up the agreement signed by the two companies in May.

Key commercial terms for the 20-year charter agreement include net charter hire to Golar (NASDAQ:GLNG) of $400M/year, plus a commodity linked tariff component of 25% of free on board prices in excess of $8/MMBtu.

The FLNG, currently under conversion in China, will sail to Argentina following its redelivery, with contract start-up expected in 2028.

The MK II FLNG will be moored in the San Matías Gulf near the FLNG Hilli, which is expected to start its 20-year charter with Southern Energy in 2027; combined, the two units have a nameplate capacity of 5.95M tons/year.

Southern Energy S.A., a company formed to enable LNG exports from Argentina, is owned by a consortium of Argentinian gas producers - Pan American Energy (OTCQB:PAANF), YPF (YPF), Pampa Energia (PAM) and Harbour Energy (OTCPK:PMOIF), plus Golar (GLNG), which holds 10%.

The FID of the MK II FLNG "solidifies $8B of net earnings visibility over 20 years to Golar, with attractive upside potential in the FLNG commodity tariff component and through our shareholding in SESA," Golar (GLNG) CEO Karl Fredrik Staubo said.

data.title: FDA lifts pause on Valneva's chikungunya vaccine in elderly
data.description:
Valneva SE (NASDAQ:VALN) said on Thursday that the FDA has lifted its pause on the use of its chikungunya vaccine, Ixchiq, for individuals aged 60 and older and has updated the prescribing information for the vaccine.

This decision follows a recommendation from the European Medicines Agency in July, which suggested removing temporary restrictions for older adults after a thorough safety review of Ixchiq.

The updated prescribing information now includes details about serious adverse events reported mainly among elderly individuals with multiple health issues, which occurred after a mass vaccination campaign in La Réunion due to a chikungunya outbreak.

The FDA has indicated that the risk of chikungunya virus exposure is low for most U.S. travelers, making the vaccine unadvisable for them. Ixchiq should only be administered when there is a significant risk of infection, and after carefully weighing the benefits and risks, the agency  noted.

Ixchiq is indicated in the U.S. against chikungunya virus in individuals 18 years of age and older who are at high risk of exposure.

data.title: Nasdaq leads charge as Trump's chip tariffs come with broad carve-outs
data.description:
Wall Street gained on Thursday, led by the Nasdaq Composite, as traders weighed President Donald Trump's latest chip tariffs, softened by sweeping exemptions.

S&P 500 (SP500) +0.5%, the Dow (DJI) +0.5%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) +0.8%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was unchanged at 4.24%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 3.73%.

Trump said Wednesday he plans to impose a 100% tariff on semiconductor imports, with an exemption for companies that manufacture or commit to manufacturing in the U.S.

Shares of Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) clinched a record high on Thursday after Taiwan said its top chip manufacturer will not have to pay a 100% tariff on such imports to the U.S.

"U.S. manufacturers live in confusing times. Alongside the widespread increases in taxes on imports that took effect this morning, Trump suggested a 100% tax on imports of microchips. However, in a maneuver that was less a retreat and more a pirouette, these taxes will not apply to imports from some specific companies, including some of the largest exporters to the U.S.," UBS' Paul Donovan said. 

On the economic side, the initial jobless claims jumped more than expected and the continuing claims rose to the highest mark since 2021

Labor cost growth cooled markedly in Q2, while productivity rose more than expected.

In more tariff news, Trump hiked the tariff on Indian goods to 50% from 25%, citing India's continued importation of Russian oil.

U.S. stocks rebounded and closed higher on Wednesday, helped by a surge in Apple (AAPL). Traders waded through a slew of quarterly reports from several big companies.

"Tech stocks have continued to drive a buoyant mood in markets, with the Mag-7 reaching a new all-time high. Momentum was also supported by strong earnings and rising hopes of imminent rate cuts as Federal Reserve speakers turned more dovish in response to last Friday’s weak payroll print," Deutsche Bank's Peter Sidorov said.

The upbeat tone has continued overnight despite Trump outlining a plan for 100% tariffs on semiconductors, with the impact of these mitigated by carveouts, Sidorov added.

The June Wholesale Inventories report will land during market hours. No revision is expected in the revised figure from an increase of 0.2 percent in the flash.

The Fed balance sheet will land later in the day.

data.title: Kelly announces selection of Chris Layden as CEO
data.description:
• Kelly (NASDAQ:KELYA) on Thursday appointed Chris Layden as president and chief executive officer, effective September 2.
  • Layden will succeed Peter Quigley, who announced his intent to retire as president and CEO.
  • Quigley will remain as a strategic advisor to Kelly to ensure a smooth transition and will continue to serve as a member of the board of directors until the company’s next Annual Shareholders Meeting in May 2026.
  • Most recently, Layden served as COO of Prolink. Before joining Prolink, Layden spent nearly two decades at ManpowerGroup, serving in a range of senior roles spanning general management, regional leadership, corporate strategy, and sales.
  • KELYA +2.04% premarket to $12.5.
  • Source: Press Release
